claim: VIOLATION: earlier.function and later.function are always identical within a group
smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 120
; VIOLATION: earlier.function and later.function are always identical within a group
;            but sourceContract vs targetContract labels suggest different functions —
;            P2 description "State mutation between X and Y" is misleading when X == Y

(declare-const earlier_function Int)   ; hash/id of earlier.function string
(declare-const later_function Int)     ; hash/id of later.function string
(declare-const same_group Int)         ; 1 = both contracts are in the same byFunction group

; Transition: grouping key = file + ":" + function
; If both are in the same group, they have identical file and function values
(assert (= same_group 1))
(assert (=> (= same_group 1) (= earlier_function later_function)))

; Transition: description reads `${earlier.function}:${earlier.line} and ${later.function}:${later.line}`
; Both function names are the same value — the description implies cross-function which is false
(declare-const description_implies_different_functions Int)  ; 1 = reader infers different fns
(assert (= description_implies_different_functions 1))

; Violation: description misleads — functions are identical, not distinct
(assert (= earlier_function later_function))
(assert (= description_implies_different_functions 1))

(check-sat)
